channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI, Google AI data centers are under stress after new genAI model launches
item.description: New generative AI (genAI) models introduced this week by Google and OpenAI have put the companies’ data centers under stress — and both companies are trying to catch up to demand.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A future Apple Watch could debut a glass frame with touch controls
item.description: A new leak and patent indicate that Apple is working on a future Apple Watch with a touchscreen glass frame.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Here's when Samsung's One UI 7 upgrade will finally roll out to your older Galaxy devices
item.description: We've got more details on when Samsung's take on Android 15 will reach the Galaxy S23 phones and other devices.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is a sequel to my favorite 3DS game, and I think it's already packing the charm that inZOI lacks
item.description: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream doesn't need inZOI's impressive visual fidelity to be the better game - here's why.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Signal chat discussion of 'clearly classified' information violated law, experts say
item.description: The inadvertent inclusion of a journalist on a Signal group chat discussing attack plans means officials likely violated the Espionage Act and public records laws while flouting guidance on how to discuss sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Researchers claim their protocol can create truly random numbers on a current quantum computer
item.description: A team that included researchers at a US bank says it has created a protocol that can generate certified truly random numbers, opening the possibility that current generation quantum computers can be used for secure applications in finance, cryptography, cybersecurity, and privacy.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The ‘New York Times’’s copyright lawsuit against OpenAI can proceed, says a federal judge
item.description: A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: With the rise of genAI, it’s time to follow Apple’s Security Recommendations
item.description: Apple’s Safari browser has a really useful password management feature, which is now also available as a standalone app called Passwords.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: HP's ridiculously fast Ryzen AI Max+ Pro 395 laptop with 128GB RAM goes on sale everywhere in the US, but it won't be cheap
item.description: HP ZBook Ultra G1a delivers AI-powered performance with a 16-core Ryzen AI Max+ Pro 395, 128GB RAM, and a high-speed 2TB SSD.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Earth’s storage of water in soil, lakes and rivers is dwindling. And it’s especially bad for farming
item.description: New research finds that global warming has significantly reduced the amount of water that’s being stored around the world in soil, lakes, rivers, snow and other places on land.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Signal defends app security amid group chat controversy
item.description: The president of Signal defended the security of the encrypted messaging app on Tuesday after a bombshell report revealed top Trump administration officials used the app to discuss airstrike plans hours before they took place.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Support for TikTok ban plunged over past 2 years: Poll
item.description: The number of Americans who support banning TikTok has fallen dramatically over the past two years, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Commercial innovations to drive FedRAMP’s new approach
item.description: Pete Waterman, the director of the cloud security program known as FedRAMP, said four new community working groups will help give industry a bigger voice. The post Commercial innovations to drive FedRAMP’s new approach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: The Apple rumor machine cranks into gear for iOS 19
item.description: WWDC event marketing intensifies, and as we head toward that event in a little over two months, it looks as if we’re being told to expect a new paint job (aka user interface change)s for iOS.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI study buddies are boosting grades to new heights
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oracle releases ML-optimized GraalVM for JDK 24
item.description: Oracle has released GraalVM for JDK 24, an alternative Java Development Kit tuned to just-released JDK 24 that uses ML (machine learning)-based profile inference to boost peak performance by about 7.9% on average on microservices benchmarks, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Improving Zero Trust Architecture through Data Management
item.description: Federal agencies must address their data management challenges to strengthen zero trust architecture, says Carmelo McCutcheon, public sector CTO for VAST Data Federal.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Striking the balance between information sharing and security
item.description: That balancing act requires a complex response, especially when designing networks and systems for collaboration. The post Striking the balance between information sharing and security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple Hosts Online WWDC 2025: What to Expect
item.description: WWDC 2025 is an online event where Apple will reveal upcoming enhancements to its operating systems, developer tools, and hints at hardware news.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 big takeaways from Nvidia GTC
item.description: Now that the dust has settled from Nvidia’s GTC 2025, a few industry experts weighed in on some core big picture developments from the conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation
